summary: Moved many HSD/sysdolphin baselib function declarations out of the global include/functions.h catch-all and into more specific baselib headers, adding several small missing headers where needed. The PR also updated source files to include the newly responsible headers directly, reducing reliance on transitive declarations from functions.h. Review discussion clarified include-placement policy: keep includes at the top for confirmed split files such as HSD, but mid-file includes can be acceptable in unsplit Melee files when they reflect future split boundaries.
what_changed: Removed HSD-related declaration groups from include/functions.h, including hsd_tobj, hsd_shadow, hsd_cobj, hsd_pobj, hsd_displayfunc, hsd_initialize, hsd_video, hsd_rumble, hsd_spline, hsd_mtx, hsd_id, hsd_quatlib, hsd_debug, hsd_gobjproc, hsd_gobjplink, and hsd_random.

Added the removed declarations to more local baselib headers such as cobj.h, debug.h, displayfunc.h, id.h, initialize.h, mtx.h, pobj.h, tobj.h, video.h, and a new include/sysdolphin/baselib/quatlib.h.
Added new small source-local baselib headers under src/sysdolphin/baselib for gobjplink.h, gobjproc.h, rumble.h, and spline.h, each carrying declarations for the corresponding HSD functions.

Updated users to include the specific baselib headers they rely on, for example ground.c includes gobjplink.h, ifstatus.c includes gobjproc.h and random.h, lbunknown_001.c includes quatlib.h, cobj.c includes displayfunc.h, fobj.c/jobj.c/wobj.c include spline.h, and aobj.c/initialize.c include debug.h.

Reordered some include blocks, including moving debug.c from including "global.h" to including <sysdolphin/baselib/debug.h> and adjusting jobj.h include order to include <dolphin/mtx.h> and explicit baselib dependencies.
smart_moves: Reduced the role of include/functions.h as a monolithic prototype sink by relocating HSD declarations to subsystem-specific headers.
Made source dependencies more explicit, which helps future decomp work avoid accidental success from unrelated global prototypes.
Created narrow headers for small or not-yet-broadly-public baselib areas such as gobjproc, gobjplink, rumble, and spline instead of leaving their declarations in a global header.

Kept declarations near their owning subsystem, making it easier to identify which baselib component owns symbols like HSD_CObjGetFar, HSD_IDSetup, HSD_VIInit, HSD_ImageDescAlloc, HSD_Panic, and splArcLengthPoint.
Addressed review concern by keeping HSD/confirmed-split include placement at the top where practical, while documenting an exception for unsplit files.
decomp_lessons: When removing declarations from functions.h, expect to add direct includes at each callsite or implementation file that was relying on transitive visibility.
For HSD/sysdolphin code, prefer component headers over a shared global declaration file; this clarifies ownership and helps future file splitting.
Confirmed split files should generally keep includes at the top because their file boundaries are known and conventional include hygiene is feasible.
For unsplit Melee files, review accepted that includes may appear near the code that will eventually be split, if that placement reflects planned future split boundaries.
Header cleanup PRs can be matching-relevant even without algorithm changes because include order and declaration visibility can affect decompilation builds and future symbol organization.
Replacing broad includes such as "global.h" with the owning module header can make dependencies more precise, as seen in src/sysdolphin/baselib/debug.c.
naming_conventions: Baselib public include guards use paths like INCLUDE_SYSDOLPHIN_BASELIB_QUATLIB_H for include/sysdolphin/baselib/quatlib.h.
New source-local baselib headers used guards like SYSDOLPHIN_BASELIB_GOBJPROC_H, SYSDOLPHIN_BASELIB_GOBJPLINK_H, SYSDOLPHIN_BASELIB_RUMBLE_H, and SYSDOLPHIN_BASELIB_SPLINE_H.
HSD subsystem symbols retain prefixes that indicate ownership, such as HSD_CObj*, HSD_PObj*, HSD_ID*, HSD_VI*, HSD_Mtx*, and HSD_ImageDesc*.
Unknown or not-yet-named functions remained as func_803xxxxx declarations and were moved to the best-known owning header rather than renamed without evidence.
Spline functions use existing lowercase-style names such as splGetHelmite and splArcLengthPoint.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: No direct assembly matching tactic was discussed in the PR excerpt.
The diff preserved existing asm/non-asm conditional structure in cobj.c around HSD_CObjEraseScreen while only adding include dependencies and whitespace.
The main matching-adjacent tactic was dependency isolation: remove broad prototypes from functions.h and add precise headers so future matched files compile against their real dependencies.

review_feedback.author: r-burns
review_feedback.feedback: Some added includes were after function declarations and should be moved up.

review_feedback.author: ribbanya
review_feedback.feedback: Author agreed HSD includes could be moved up, but asked whether the same rule should apply to unsplit Melee files that already mix includes with definitions.

review_feedback.author: r-burns
review_feedback.feedback: Reviewer preferred includes at the top for files that are not already mixed, but accepted leaving already-mixed files as-is.

review_feedback.author: ribbanya
review_feedback.feedback: Author distinguished confirmed splits, which HSD mostly or entirely consists of, from less-certain Melee unsplit files.

review_feedback.author: r-burns
review_feedback.feedback: Reviewer accepted mid-file include placement for files that will definitely need to be split later, because the includes may be roughly where they belong after splitting.

follow_up_queries: Find later PRs that continue removing declarations from include/functions.h.
Search for remaining HSD declarations still present in include/functions.h after PR 644.
Check whether src/sysdolphin/baselib/gobjproc.h, gobjplink.h, rumble.h, and spline.h were later moved from src/ to include/.
Find review discussions about include placement in unsplit Melee files versus confirmed split HSD files.
Search for subsequent cleanup of HSD_Randf and HSD_Randi declarations after their functions.h group was removed.
